method: engine_kinds = unique `X.kind === '...'` string comparisons grepped from real_battle_simulator.html (pattern: kind === '[^']+'), excluding damage-chip kinds 'eff-up'/'eff-down' (a separate UI-chip kind namespace, not move effects). A move's effect kind counts as supported iff that exact string literal appears as a kind===  comparison somewhere in the engine source.
caveats: 急所率上昇 (high-crit-rate effect) is never read via kind==='急所率上昇' anywhere in the engine; instead the engine reads a separate top-level field move.battle_data.crit_stage (see line ~2678: `_a.critBoost + (move.battle_data.crit_stage||0) + _lens`). Of the 29 moves carrying kind '急所率上昇' in this data, 15 ALSO carry a redundant crit_stage:1/0 field and so DO get high-crit in practice (e.g. ゴッドバード, クラブハンマー, クロスチョップ, リーフブレード, ストーンエッジ...); the remaining ~13 have no crit_stage field at all and so get NO high-crit bonus in the engine despite carrying the effect (e.g. からてチョップ, かまいたち, はっぱカッター, きりさく, エアロブラスト, ポイズンテール, こうげきしれい, あくうせつだん, とぎすます, １０００まんボルト, ねらいうち, オーラウイング, ツタこんぼう). This report still marks all 29 as partial/unsupported-kind because the kind string itself has no generic handler; readers should check crit_stage per-move before assuming a fix is needed.

みがわり貫通 (substitute-pierce) IS additionally readable via a parallel top-level flag move.battle_data.substitute_pierce===true (line ~3334), so some substitute-piercing moves may work even if not listed here as using kind 'みがわり貫通'. No equivalent parallel field exists for まもり貫通 (protect-piercing, 11 moves e.g. フェイント/なみだめ/シャドーダイブ) - grep for protect_pierce/ignores_protect/bypasses_protect found zero hits, so those 11 are a genuine, unmitigated gap.

すなあらし (Sandstorm, the move) shows unsupported kinds 能力倍率/全体継続ダメージ, but the actual in-battle mechanics (sand rock-spdef boost, per-turn sand chip damage) are implemented separately via a global env.weather==='sand' system (phaseSlip, damage calc), not by reading these per-move effect kinds. The move itself works (weather-set via kind 天候変化, which IS engine-supported); the two flagged kinds are descriptive/redundant, not a functional gap.
